name: Paracetamol
use: Used to relieve mild to moderate pain and reduce fever.
precautions: Do not exceed the recommended dose. Avoid alcohol. Can harm the liver in high doses or with long-term heavy use.

name: Ibuprofen
use: A pain reliever and anti-inflammatory used for pain, swelling, and fever.
precautions: Take with food to avoid stomach upset. Avoid if you have stomach ulcers, kidney problems, or are in late pregnancy.

name: Amoxicillin
use: An antibiotic used to treat bacterial infections.
precautions: Complete the full course even if you feel better. Not effective against viral infections like cold or flu. May cause allergic reactions in penicillin-sensitive people.

name: Azithromycin
use: An antibiotic used to treat a range of bacterial infections including respiratory and skin infections.
precautions: Complete the full course. May interact with heart medications. Avoid antacids within 2 hours of taking it.

name: Cetirizine
use: An antihistamine used to relieve allergy symptoms like sneezing, itching, and runny nose.
precautions: May cause drowsiness. Avoid alcohol. Use caution when driving or operating machinery.

name: Omeprazole
use: Reduces stomach acid, used for acidity, heartburn, and ulcers.
precautions: Long-term use may affect nutrient absorption. Take before meals for best effect. Consult a doctor if used beyond 2 weeks without relief.

name: Metformin
use: Used to control blood sugar levels in type 2 diabetes.
precautions: Take with food to reduce stomach upset. Requires regular blood sugar monitoring. Not for type 1 diabetes.

name: Amlodipine
use: Used to treat high blood pressure and chest pain (angina).
precautions: May cause swelling in ankles or dizziness. Do not stop suddenly without consulting a doctor.

name: Metronidazole
use: An antibiotic used to treat certain bacterial and parasitic infections.
precautions: Avoid alcohol during and for 48 hours after treatment, it can cause severe reactions. Complete the full course.

name: Ranitidine
use: Reduces stomach acid, used for acidity and ulcers.
precautions: Consult a doctor for prolonged use. Some formulations have been recalled in various countries; check current safety notices.

name: Diclofenac
use: A pain reliever and anti-inflammatory used for joint and muscle pain.
precautions: Take with food. Avoid if you have heart, kidney, or stomach ulcer conditions. Not recommended in late pregnancy.

name: Loratadine
use: An antihistamine used for allergy relief with less drowsiness than older antihistamines.
precautions: Generally non-drowsy but avoid alcohol. Consult a doctor if pregnant or breastfeeding.

name: Pantoprazole
use: Reduces stomach acid, used for acidity, GERD, and ulcers.
precautions: Best taken on an empty stomach before breakfast. Long-term use should be monitored by a doctor.

name: Aspirin
use: Used for pain relief, fever, and in low doses to prevent blood clots.
precautions: Avoid in children with viral infections (risk of Reye's syndrome). Can increase bleeding risk. Take with food.

name: Domperidone
use: Used to relieve nausea, vomiting, and bloating.
precautions: Avoid in heart rhythm conditions. Do not exceed recommended dose or duration.
